achyutashtakam: Invokes the blessings of Lord Vishnu in all His forms
Traditionally associated with devotion and love towards the supreme lord
Recited to wash away accumulated sins and lighten the burden of negative karma
Invokes peace of mind and spiritual joy
Traditionally invoked for protection from all dangers and evil influences
Invokes the blessing of liberation when recited with pure devotion
ananthashayana-stotram: Invokes the cosmic protective form of Vishnu as Ananthashayana
Invokes restful sleep and removes nightmares
Draws the abundance of Lakshmi who serves Vishnu on the cosmic ocean
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of fears and provides divine security
Powerful for Ekadashi and Anant Chaturdashi observance
Traditionally recited to invoke Vishnu's constant vigilance over the devotee
anjaneya-dandakam: Comprehensive prayer narrating Hanuman's heroic deeds in continuous prose-verse form
Invokes fulfillment of all desires when recited at dawn
Invokes the blessing of long life, good health, prosperity and strength
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of fears and obstacles from the devotee's life
Associated in tradition with diseases and protects from evil forces
Chanted on Hanuman's Tuesdays and Saturdays for his swift intervention
anjaneya-stotram: Powerful Anjaneya (Hanuman) prayer for complete protection
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins and bestows success in all endeavors
Traditionally recited for removal of fear of enemies and evil forces
Draws on Hanuman's legendary strength, courage, and clear discernment
Especially potent on Tuesdays and Saturdays
Traditionally associated with victory in all undertakings
annapurna-ashtakam: Composed by Adi Shankaracharya, one of the most celebrated ashtakams
Invokes Annapurna Devi of Kashi for food, nourishment, and spiritual sustenance
Invokes jnana (knowledge) and vairagya (detachment) along with material needs
Recited to drive away poverty, hunger, and every form of scarcity
Opens the door to moksha (liberation) through divine grace
Each verse culminates in the powerful prayer 'Bhiksham Dehi' (give alms)
annapurna-stotram-shankaracharya: Shankaracharya's five-verse hymn to Annapurna, the goddess who feeds all creation
Calls on Annapurna of Kashi to fill the home with food and abundance
Seeks the mother's grace for a household free of want and hunger
Recited to dissolve the karma of scarcity and earn her nourishing mercy
Chanted on Annapurna Jayanti and through Navratri for her sustaining blessings
ashtavinayak-stotram: Honours the eight sacred Ganpati shrines of Maharashtra in a single prayer
Calls on all eight Vinayaka forms to clear obstacles from every venture
Invokes Ganesha's grace for safe pilgrimage to the Ashtavinayak temples
Recited to remove hindrances and earn success before new undertakings
Chanted on Wednesdays and during Ganesh Chaturthi for the lord's favour
ayushya-suktam: Powerful Vedic prayer for long life and longevity
Invokes threefold lifespan (tryayusham) like sages Jamadagni and Kashyapa
Recited to shield against untimely death and fatal accidents
Invokes Savita (Sun) from all four directions for complete protection
Traditionally recited for removal of sins and negative karma that shorten life
Ideal for chanting during birthday ceremonies and life milestones
Traditionally recited for newborns and the elderly alike
ayyappa-pancharatnam: Five gem-verses praising Lord Ayyappa, the son of Hari and Hara
Invokes Ayyappa as Dharma Shasta who unites Shiva and Vishnu
Sought for the lord's protection along the Sabarimala forest path
Recited to purify the heart during the disciplined vrat of devotion
Chanted by pilgrims on Saturdays and through the Mandala Puja season
ayyappa-sahasranama: Invokes the thousand names and blessings of Lord Ayyappa
Invokes moksha (eternal liberation) to devoted reciters
Especially powerful during Mandala Kalam (41-day vrat period)
Recited to cleanse every sin and purify the soul before pilgrimage
Invokes the blessing of sarva-siddhi (all spiritual accomplishments)
Invokes the spiritual merit of Sabarimala pilgrimage
badrinath-aarti: Invokes the blessing of the merit of Badrinath Dham pilgrimage - one of the Char Dhams
Sought for moksha and release from the cycle of birth and death
Invokes Lord Vishnu as Badri Vishal for divine protection
Purifies all sins through the grace of Badrinath
Performed during the sacred opening season of Badrinath temple
badrinath-stotram: Equivalent to pilgrimage to Badrinath Dham
Invokes Vaikuntha (the supreme abode of Vishnu)
Invokes the blessing of the blessings of Nara-Narayana
Recited to cleanse sins and earn the purifying grace of Badri Vishal
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of righteous desires
Invokes the blessing of supreme knowledge and liberation
bajrang-baan: Tulsidas's urgent invocation summoning Hanuman as an arrow against evil
Traditionally recited for removal of fear and grants immense courage
Commands Hanuman to strike down enemies and clear life's obstacles
Associated in tradition with diseases caused by negative energies
Invokes immediate relief in times of extreme distress
Invokes the blessing of the divine protection of Lord Hanuman
bhagavad-gita-dhyana-shloka: Traditionally invoked to establish the perfect meditative state before Gita study
Invokes the blessings of Vyasa and Krishna
Recited to dispel inner ignorance and ripen spiritual wisdom
Purifies the mind for receiving divine knowledge
Traditionally recited for removal of sins accumulated in Kali Yuga
Essential preparation for Bhagavad Gita recitation
bhagavad-gita-dhyanam: Meditative invocation that opens recitation of the Bhagavad Gita
Salutes Krishna as the cowherd guru who revealed the Gita to Arjuna
Honours Mother Gita as the bestower of liberating knowledge
Recited to steady the mind and absorb the Gita's wisdom
Chanted on Gita Jayanti and Wednesdays sacred to Lord Krishna
bhairav-aarti: Calls on fierce Bhairava to shield devotees from evil spirits and black magic
Traditionally recited for removal of Shani, Rahu, and Ketu dosha effects
Invokes fearlessness and guards against theft and crime
Essential aarti for Kaal Bhairav Ashtami worship
Invokes the fierce protective aspect of Lord Shiva
bhairav-kavacham: Traditionally invoked to establish a divine protective armor around the devotee through Bhairav's grace
Shields every part of the body from head to toe with Bhairav's protection
Guards against evil spirits, black magic, and occult attacks
Traditionally recited for removal of fear in dangerous places including battlefields and lonely areas
Bestows the tantric siddhis attained through Bhairava's fierce grace
Especially effective when recited during Kalashtami or at midnight
bhairava-dwadash-nama: Twelve forms of Bhairava invoked for complete protection
Morning recitation that frees the devotee from accumulated sins
Traditionally invoked for removal of fear of thieves and enemies
Bhairava himself protects the devotee at all times
bhairava-hridayam: Heart hymn of Lord Bhairava for supreme protection
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of fears, diseases, and sorrows
Invokes Bhairava as Kshetrapala for place protection
Invokes victory and freedom from all afflictions
Powerful for Kala Bhairava Ashtami and Saturday worship
bhairava-sahasranama: Thousand names of Lord Bhairava the fierce form of Shiva
Supreme protection from all evil spirits and negative forces
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of planetary afflictions and Yama's punishments
Invokes all eight forms of Bhairava simultaneously
Essential for Kala Bhairava Ashtami and Saturday worship
bhimashankar-stotram: Equivalent to pilgrimage to Bhimashankar, the sixth Jyotirlinga
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins and negative influences
Traditionally invoked for protection from evil spirits and dakini afflictions
Invokes health, removing disease and sorrow
Grants the perfections that Shiva bestows at this Jyotirlinga shrine
Leads the devout toward Shiva-sayujya, merging with the lord himself
bhuvaneswari-stotram: Tantric hymn to Bhuvaneswari, the goddess who rules all the worlds
Invokes the queen of the universe to grant her sovereign grace
Seeks the Devi's protection and the prosperity she commands over creation
Recited to ripen inner sadhana and rise in spiritual attainment
Chanted on Fridays and through Navratri for the mother's favour
bilvarchana-stotram: Glorifies the supreme merit of offering Bilva leaves to Shiva
One Bilva leaf offering equals crores of Kanyadaan
Recited so the offering wipes away the sins of three lifetimes
Invokes the fruit of visiting Kashi and Prayag
Invokes the blessing of Shivaloka (abode of Shiva) upon the devotee
Especially powerful during Shravan month and Maha Shivaratri
bilvashtakam: Glorifies the Bilva leaf as the dearest offering at Shiva's feet
Offering even one Bilva leaf to Shiva equals great donations
Merit equivalent to Kashi pilgrimage and Ashvamedha Yagna
Ideal recitation during Bilva patra offering on Mondays
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of karmic debts and negative effects
Enhances the spiritual merit of Shiva puja manifold
brahma-dwadash-nama: Twelve names of Lord Brahma, the creator of the universe
Invokes the creator's mastery over every branch of knowledge and learning
Enhances creative power, intelligence, and wisdom
Invokes the blessing of happiness through progeny and family
Traditionally recited to invoke continuous grace of Brahma Deva
